title: eriophyllum wallacei subsp. australe
intro: Eriophyllum wallacei subsp. australe, commonly known as a coastal woolly sunflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family native to the coastal regions of southwestern Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Eriophyllum wallacei subsp. australe include its potential to reduce inflammation, soothe skin conditions, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
